# spacelift_intent_project (Resource)

`spacelift_intent_project` represents a Spacelift **Intent project** - a workspace for managing cloud resources through Spacelift Intent. An Intent project can be given a time-to-live (TTL) after which its resources are automatically cleaned up.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "spacelift_intent_project" "sandbox" {
  name             = "Ephemeral sandbox"
  space_id         = "root"
  description      = "Sandbox environment, cleaned up automatically after three days"
  labels           = ["intent", "sandbox"]
  ttl              = "72h"
  on_expiry_action = "DELETE"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the intent project
- `space_id` (String) ID (slug) of the space the intent project is in

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form intent project description for users
- `expires_at` (Number) Absolute Unix timestamp at which the intent project's TTL elapses and resource cleanup kicks in. Mutually exclusive with `ttl`.
- `keep_resources_on_destroy` (Boolean) Keep the intent project's managed cloud resources when the project is destroyed. Defaults to `false`.
- `labels` (Set of String) Labels of the intent project, used for policy autoattachment and filtering
- `on_expiry_action` (String) What happens to the project after TTL expiry cleanup. Possible values are `ARCHIVE` (keep the project in a read-only, archived state) and `DELETE` (delete the project entirely). Defaults to `ARCHIVE`.
- `runner_image` (String) Optional Docker image used to run intent tasks for this project. Leave empty to use the system default intent worker image.
- `ttl` (String) Time-to-live for the intent project expressed as a duration (e.g. `72h`, `7d`, `30d`). When the TTL elapses, the project's resources are cleaned up according to `on_expiry_action`. Mutually exclusive with `expires_at`.
- `worker_pool_id` (String) ID of the worker pool assigned to this intent project for task execution. When omitted, the default public worker pool is assigned by the backend.

### Read-Only

- `archived_at` (Number) Unix timestamp when the project was archived by TTL expiry cleanup.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `state` (String) The state of the intent project.
- `ttl_seconds` (Number) The originally configured TTL duration in seconds. Zero when the TTL was set as an absolute timestamp (or not at all).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import spacelift_intent_project.sandbox $INTENT_PROJECT_ID
```
